ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general chicken soup class (FOODON_03311737) but no specific Thai Tom Yum variant. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP covers chicken soup generically. No specific Tom Yum or Thai spiced chicken soup code exists in FoodEx2; closest parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept exists for Tom Yum chicken soup. SNOMED CT does not enumerate individual dish variants at this level of specificity.

preparation_method: spiced with lemongrass, galangal, kaffir lime leaves, chilli, and fish sauce

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bowl of Red Tom Yum Chicken Soup as served in a Thai restaurant, approximately 350 g, containing broth, chicken pieces, mushrooms, and aromatic herbs.

nutrient_source.title: Tom Yum Soup (Chicken) — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite, cross-referenced with FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table for Southeast Asian dishes
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Red Tom Yum Chicken Soup exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN. Values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central data for chicken-based soups (NDB No. 06172, chicken broth and soup composites) combined with FAO/INFOODS SEA regional data for Tom Yum soup variants. The 'red' variant typically incorporates red curry paste and may include a small amount of coconut milk, slightly elevating fat and energy relative to clear Tom Yum. Nutrient values represent a best-estimate composite for a restaurant-prepared red Tom Yum chicken soup at approximately 5–6% chicken by weight in broth.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of mixed dishes and soups — analogous estimate based on chicken-vegetable soup and low-GI broth-based dishes
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Red Tom Yum Chicken Soup is listed in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI is estimated at 38 (low) based on analogous broth-based chicken soups and mixed vegetable soups documented in the University of Sydney GI Database, which consistently show GI values in the 30–45 range. The low carbohydrate content (3.2 g per 100 g) and high water content further support a low GI classification. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (38 × 11.2) / 100 ≈ 4.3, where carbohydrate per 350 g serving = 3.2 × 3.5 = 11.2 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from fish sauce and broth
hypertension.guidance: A 350 g serving of Red Tom Yum Chicken Soup provides approximately 1470 mg of sodium, representing over 60% of the WHO-recommended daily limit of 2000 mg; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size, request reduced fish sauce, and avoid adding additional condiments.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate content and low glycemic index
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a low glycemic index of approximately 38 and only 11.2 g of carbohydrate per standard 350 g serving, Red Tom Yum Chicken Soup produces a low glycemic load of approximately 4.3 and is generally suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, provided sodium intake is monitored concurrently.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium and moderate saturated fat from red curry paste and optional coconut milk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The high sodium content (~420 mg per 100 g) poses a cardiovascular risk with regular consumption; the saturated fat contribution from red curry paste and any coconut milk should be considered, and individuals with cardiovascular disease are advised to choose the clear (non-red) Tom Yum variant or request preparation without coconut milk to reduce saturated fat intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Red Tom Yum Chicken Soup (ต้มยำไก่แดง; 红冬阴鸡肉汤) is a Thai hot-and-sour soup made with chicken, aromatic herbs, and a red-spiced broth. Unlike the clear Tom Yum variant, the red version incorporates red curry paste and sometimes a small amount of coconut milk, giving the broth a reddish hue, a slightly richer body, and a more complex spice profile. The soup is built on a base of lemongrass, galangal, kaffir lime leaves, Thai bird's eye chillies, fish sauce, and lime juice — the classic Tom Yum aromatics — with the addition of red curry paste deepening the colour and intensifying the heat. Chicken pieces (typically breast or thigh) are simmered in the broth until tender, and mushrooms, tomatoes, and sometimes straw mushrooms are added for texture and umami. The flavour profile is simultaneously sour, spicy, savoury, and faintly sweet, with a pronounced herbal fragrance from the lemongrass and kaffir lime. Nutritionally, the soup is relatively low in calories (~52 kcal per 100 g) and carbohydrates (~3.2 g per 100 g), making it a low-glycemic-index dish (estimated GI ~38) suitable for individuals managing blood glucose. It provides a moderate amount of protein (~4.8 g per 100 g) from the chicken. The primary clinical concern is its high sodium content (~420 mg per 100 g), largely from fish sauce and broth, which is relevant for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. This dish is commonly served in Thai restaurants across Singapore, including at Sanook Kitchen, and is popular as a light but flavourful main course or starter.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Red Tom Yum Chicken Soup was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or IMR Malaysia MyFCD. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central chicken soup composites and FAO/INFOODS SEA regional data. The GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database based on comparable broth-based chicken soups. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central (chicken soup composites, NDB No. 06172 and related entries) and FAO/INFOODS SEA food composition data for Tom Yum soup variants. No exact match was found in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN. 2. The 'red' variant of Tom Yum uses red curry paste and may include coconut milk; this slightly elevates fat, saturated fat, and energy compared to the clear Tom Yum variant. Values reflect a typical restaurant preparation. 3. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA chicken soup composite entries used as the primary nutrient reference. 4. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the primary source composite for this soup type. 5. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the FAO/INFOODS SEA or USDA composite entries used. 6. Trans fat is reported as 0.0; this is confirmed zero per USDA data for chicken-based soups with no hydrogenated fat ingredients. 7. GI is an analogous estimate of 38 (low) based on University of Sydney GI Database data for broth-based chicken and vegetable soups. 8. Sodium content is high due to fish sauce; individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease should be advised accordingly. 9. The food_id GD001189 and restaurant attribution (Sanook Kitchen - Funan, field survey data) are preserved from the input seed record. 10. The input food_name '211D Red Tom Yum Chicken Soup 红冬阴鸡肉汤' has been canonicalized to 'Red Tom Yum Chicken Soup'; the menu code '211D' and Chinese characters are preserved in aliases.
